perturbation.original_text: All actions and decisions with respect to binding the Joint Venture in contract requires the consent of a majority of the Participants.
perturbation.changed_text: All actions and decisions with respect to binding the Joint Venture in contract requires the consent of all of the Participants.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity because Section 19 now states that all contract binding authority requires unanimous consent, contradicting Section 18 which states that most management decisions only require a majority vote. This could cause disputes over whether a particular action related to a contract needs a simple majority or full agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as all of the Participants may otherwise agree in writing, all actions and decisions respecting the management, operation and control of the Joint Venture and its business will be decided by a majority vote of the Participants.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the requirement for binding the Joint Venture in contract from a majority consent to unanimous consent. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states that most management decisions only require a majority vote. This introduces a clear in-text contradiction because it creates uncertainty about whether contract-related actions need a simple majority or full agreement, which qualifies as a strong contradiction based on the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: A Participant will not be liable to the Joint Venture, or to any other Participant, for any mistake or error in judgment or for any act or omission done in good faith and believed to be within the scope of authority conferred or implied by this Agreement or the Joint Venture.
perturbation.changed_text: A Participant will be liable to the Joint Venture, or to any other Participant, for any mistake or error in judgment or for any act or omission done in bad faith and believed to be outside the scope of authority conferred or implied by this Agreement or the Joint Venture.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding liability. The original text provides protection for actions within the scope of authority done in good faith. The modified text asserts that participants are liable for actions done outside the scope of conferred authority in bad faith. This contradicts the earlier indemnification clause (Section 55) that states Participants will be indemnified and held harmless by the Joint Venture from and against any and all claims of any nature. Which states that the Particpant will not be liable for the acitons that are being made. This would now need to be proven in order to hold the other party liable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Participants will be indemnified and held harmless by the Joint Venture from and against any and all claims of any nature, whatsoever, arising out of a Participant's participation in Joint Venture affairs. A Participant will not be entitled to indemnification under this section for liability arising out of gross negligence or wil l ful misconduct of the Participant or the breach by the Participant of any provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states a participant is NOT liable for errors in judgment done in good faith within the scope of authority. The changed text now makes a participant liable for actions done in bad faith outside the scope of authority. This directly contradicts the section stating that 'All Participants will be indemnified and held harmless by the Joint Venture from and against any and all claims of any nature, whatsoever, arising out of a Participant's participation in Joint Venture affairs' creating an in-text contradiction as it provides an exception to the indemnification that wasn't there before the change.
